theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Technology (+1.57%) and Industrials (+0.44%) led the market today, while Health Care (-1.69%) and Consumer Staples (-1.40%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged +0.49%, defensives -1.06%, and value/cyclicals +0.09% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.38%, Dow +0.02%, Nasdaq +0.90% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at +0.39%. The VIX fell 3.79% to 16.50, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.85% to $4,056.90, crude oil was little changed at $79.75, and bitcoin gained 4.26% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
